title: Hexadecimal
description: Converting between denary, binary and hexadecimal.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick check from binary addition.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y9hex-dna',questions:[{prompt:'What is 1 + 1 in binary?',html:'

1 + 1 = ?

',answer:'10',right:'Correct.'},{prompt:'Add these numbers.',type:'addition',rowA:[0,0,0,0,0,1,1,1],rowB:[0,0,0,0,0,0,0,1],answer:'00001000',right:'7 + 1 = 8.'},{prompt:'Convert to denary.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,0,1,1,1,1]),answer:'15',right:'8 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 15.'}]});

steps.title: Hex Digits
steps.content:
Hexadecimal digits

Hexadecimal is base 16. It uses 0-9 and then A-F for values 10-15.

HexABCDEF
Denary101112131415

steps.title: Step Through: Hex to Denary
steps.content: Step through hex to denary
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({containerId:'y9hex-stepper-1',steps:[{title:'We are converting A5 to denary',html:'
A
5
',note:'The left digit is in the 16s column. The right digit is in the 1s column.'},{title:'Work out the left digit',html:'

A means 10 in denary.

10 × 16 = 160

16s column1s column
A = 10 × 16 = 160?
',note:'The left digit is in the 16s column, so multiply its denary value by 16.'},{title:'Add the right digit',html:'

The right digit 5 is in the 1s column — just add it as it is.

160 + 5 = 165

16s column1s column
A = 1605
',note:'The 1s column value is just added on without multiplying.'},{title:'A5 in denary is 165',html:'
165
',note:'Check: (10 × 16) + 5 = 160 + 5 = 165 ✓'}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Binary to Hex
steps.content: Step through binary to hex
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({containerId:'y9hex-stepper-2',steps:[{title:'We are converting 11001010 to hex',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,1,0,0,1,0,1,0], [8,4,2,1,8,4,2,1], true),note:'Split the 8 bits into two groups of 4. Each group of 4 bits becomes one hex digit.'},{title:'Convert the left group: 1100',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,1,0,0],[8,4,2,1]) + '

8 + 4 = 12 = C

',note:'Add up the active place values in the left group, just like binary to denary — but only 4 bits. 8 + 4 = 12, and 12 is C in hex.'},{title:'Convert the right group: 1010',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,0],[8,4,2,1]) + '

8 + 2 = 10 = A

',note:'Do the same for the right group. 8 + 2 = 10, and 10 is A in hex.'},{title:'Join the two hex digits',html:'
C
A
',note:'Left group gave C, right group gave A. 11001010 in hex is CA.'}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Denary to Hex
steps.content: Step through denary to hex
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({containerId:'y9hex-stepper-3',steps:[{title:'We are converting 200 to hex',html:'
200
',note:'A hex number has a left digit (worth 16s) and a right digit (worth 1s). We need to find both.'},{title:'Divide 200 by 16',html:'
200 ÷ 16= 12.5
Cut off the decimal= 12

The left digit is 12 — which is C in hex.

C
?
',note:'Divide by 16 and throw away everything after the decimal point. That number is the left hex digit. 12 is above 9, so it becomes C.'},{title:'Find the right digit',html:'

We have 12 sixteens, which is 192.

12 × 16 = 192

What is the difference between 192 and 200?

200 − 192 = 8

The right digit is 8.

C
8
',note:'8 is 9 or below, so it stays as 8 — no letter needed.'},{title:'The answer is C8',html:'
C
8
',note:'200 in hex is C8. Check: (12 × 16) + 8 = 192 + 8 = 200 ✓'}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Hex to Binary
steps.content: Step through hex to binary
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({containerId:'y9hex-stepper-4',steps:[{title:'We are converting B3 to binary',html:'
B
3
',note:'Each hex digit converts to exactly 4 bits. Handle the left digit and the right digit separately.'},{title:'Find the denary value of each digit',html:'
Hex digitDenary value
B11
33
',note:'Use the hex digit table: B = 11. The digit 3 stays as 3.'},{title:'Write each value as 4 bits',html:'
DigitDenary4 bits
B111011
330011
',note:'Always write 4 bits for each digit, even if the number is small — 3 needs a leading zero to make 0011.'},{title:'Put the two groups of 4 bits together',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,1,0,0,1,1],[8,4,2,1,8,4,2,1],true),note:'B gives the left 4 bits and 3 gives the right 4 bits. B3 in binary is 10110011.'}]});
